violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener & can opener holder) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC moved it to 3-bay sink to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was (100) parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be (200-400) ppm. PIC adjusted sanitizer in 3-bay sink during time of inspection. Sanitizer was retest at 200ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

Several pieces of equipment located in the kitchen area was not in good repair: 1.Hand sink across from prep station was observed with cold water knob not working. 2.3-bay sink observed leaking under sanitizers bay. PIC informed me that service order have been placed. PIC shall send pictures to inspector once adjustment/correction. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2024

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

Thermometers were lacking in refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2024

Utensils (extra tongs) were stored in a manner that did not prevent from miscellaneous sources of contamination (stored uncovered near open drainage system). PIC moved tongs at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2024

Employees' personal belongings were observed stored in food storage areas. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2024
